hero.title: Obsessed with Brands That Move People, Turning Data into Strategy and Ideas into Impact.
hero.intro:
I've never really done things on the typical timeline and I think that's my greatest asset.
Before I ever set foot in a college classroom, I was already out in the world. I skipped a grade, graduated at 16, and spent my gap year traveling and volunteering, collecting experiences and perspectives that no degree could have given me. I have always moved with ambition, but never without intention.
Since then, I have not stopped. Every single summer, whether it was a job or volunteering, I have shown up somewhere new. A different state, a different country, a different industry. Dallas with AT&T. New York City with L'Oréal. Portugal on an academic program. Each experience added a new lens and made me sharper, more adaptable, and more confident in rooms I had never been in before.
When I got to VCU I hit the ground running. I study Marketing, Data Analytics, and Sales, but my real education has happened in the work itself: building partnerships with Fortune 500 companies, helping launch a fellowship from the ground up, and leading a 68-member organization as Executive President.
I am obsessed with brands that make people feel something and partnerships that create real mutual value. I care about the strategy behind the story, who we are talking to, why it matters, and how to make it land. That combination of sales instinct and marketing curiosity is what drives me.

jobs.blurb: At Extern, I drive the full partnership sales cycle from prospecting to close, independently securing strategic collaborations with major global brands. I cultivate a high volume of client relationships through LinkedIn and email outreach, focused on accelerating account growth and hitting quarterly goals. I also pioneer AI agent workflows to sharpen outbound efficiency, building smarter systems for research, lead qualification, and outreach.

jobs.company: VCU Office of Innovation & Strategic Design
jobs.meta: Innovation & Strategic Design Fellow | Richmond, VA
jobs.blurb: As the inaugural Innovation and Strategic Design Fellow at VCU's Da Vinci Center, I am architecting a brand new fellowship program from the ground up. I spearhead business development through digital marketing campaigns and targeted outreach to forge partnerships with Fortune 500 companies. I also orchestrate Design Jams and lead recruitment efforts, connecting students with university leadership and state government stakeholders.

jobs.meta: Omnichannel Education & Sales Intern | New York City, NY
jobs.blurb: At L'Oréal, I spearheaded comprehensive competitive intelligence research across the beauty industry, dissecting product offerings, pricing strategies, and market positioning to shape executive-level strategy decisions. I stewarded vendor relationships and drove account operations through Salesforce CRM, and crafted a DEI business case study that championed strategic expansion into underserved market segments.

jobs.meta: Senior Associate Marketing Intern | Dallas, TX
jobs.blurb: Senior Associate Marketing Intern At AT&T, I engineered data-driven campaign recommendations using KPI analysis and financial projections to sharpen budget allocation and maximize ROI. I also conceived and produced the monthly newsletter for AT&T's Mid Markets division, covering company updates, events, and corporate social responsibility initiatives to unite internal teams and drive awareness.

items.name: Alpha Kappa Psi Fraternity
items.blurb: Lead a 68-member chapter and a 7-person executive board while managing a $10K budget and driving recruitment strategies that increased outcomes by 40%.

items.name: Student Managed Investment Portfolio (SMIP)
items.blurb: Analyze consumer staples opportunities with Bloomberg and IBISWorld research and collaborate with a 40-analyst team to support decisions for a $415K portfolio.

gapYear.title: Gap Year Journey
gapYear.blurb: Before college, I spent a year living in Hawaii and Colombia, volunteering and working with local organizations to give back to the communities that welcomed me. In Hawaii, I participated in beach and trail cleanups, working alongside conservation groups to preserve the natural spaces that locals and visitors depend on. In Colombia, I taught English through community education programs, helping open doors for people in communities where language access makes a real difference. That year taught me how to connect with people from all walks of life, how to show up for something bigger than yourself, and how to find purpose in the everyday effort of building something good. It is not something I talk about often, but it shaped who I am more than I can put into words.
